result.stdout:
qdk-chem:
  Tools for working with quantum chemistry data.

Usage:
  qdk-chem [options] [command]

Options:
  --version         Show version information
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  convert <path>      Converts between various problem description formats.
                      Note that not all data is supported by all formats, such
                      that data may be lost when converting to formats other
                      than Broombridge.
  normalize <path>    Normalizes the problem description data in a given
                      problem description fileby loading and then saving the
                      data in a given format.
  export-jw <path>    Exports a JSON representation of the Jordan–Wigner
                      transformation of the fermionic Hamiltonian for a
                      particular electronic structure problem.

commands.payload:
qdk-chem:
  Tools for working with quantum chemistry data.

Usage:
  qdk-chem [options] [command]

Options:
  --version         Show version information
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  convert <path>      Converts between various problem description formats.
                      Note that not all data is supported by all formats, such
                      that data may be lost when converting to formats other
                      than Broombridge.
  normalize <path>    Normalizes the problem description data in a given
                      problem description fileby loading and then saving the
                      data in a given format.
  export-jw <path>    Exports a JSON representation of the Jordan–Wigner
                      transformation of the fermionic Hamiltonian for a
                      particular electronic structure problem.

result.stdout:
convert:
  Converts between various problem description formats. Note that not all data
  is supported by all formats, such that data may be lost when converting to
  formats other than Broombridge.

Usage:
  qdk-chem convert [options] <path>

Arguments:
  <path>    Input data to be loaded, or - to load from stdin.

Options:
  --from <Broombridge|FciDump|LiQuiD>    Format to use in loading problem
                                         description data.
  --to <Broombridge|FciDump|LiQuiD>      Format to use in writing problem
                                         description data.
  --out <out>                            Path to write output to. Data will be
                                         written to stdout by default.
  -?, -h, --help                         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
convert:
  Converts between various problem description formats. Note that not all data
  is supported by all formats, such that data may be lost when converting to
  formats other than Broombridge.

Usage:
  qdk-chem convert [options] <path>

Arguments:
  <path>    Input data to be loaded, or - to load from stdin.

Options:
  --from <Broombridge|FciDump|LiQuiD>    Format to use in loading problem
                                         description data.
  --to <Broombridge|FciDump|LiQuiD>      Format to use in writing problem
                                         description data.
  --out <out>                            Path to write output to. Data will be
                                         written to stdout by default.
  -?, -h, --help                         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
export-jw:
  Exports a JSON representation of the Jordan–Wigner transformation of the
  fermionic Hamiltonian for a particular electronic structure problem.

Usage:
  qdk-chem export-jw [options] <path>

Arguments:
  <path>    Input data to be loaded, or - to load from stdin.

Options:
  --format <Broombridge|FciDump|LiQuiD>    Format to use in loading problem
                                           description data.
  --out <out>                              Path to write output to. Data will
                                           be written to stdout by default.
  --flatten                                If true, flattens resulting JSON
                                           (often easier for use in native
                                           code).
  -?, -h, --help                           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
normalize:
  Normalizes the problem description data in a given problem description
  fileby loading and then saving the data in a given format.

Usage:
  qdk-chem normalize [options] <path>

Arguments:
  <path>    Input data to be loaded, or - to load from stdin.

Options:
  --format <Broombridge|FciDump|LiQuiD>    Format to use in reading and
                                           writing problem description data.
  --out <out>                              Path to write output to. Data will
                                           be written to stdout by default.
  -?, -h, --help                           Show help and usage information
